demands.title: Dynamic Beam Capacity Allocation API
demands.description: Development of a REST API enabling external clients to dynamically reallocate contracted MHz capacity across satellite beams in near real-time. The system must complete end-to-end execution within 15 minutes.
demands.business_requirements: BR-01: Allow dynamic redistribution of contracted MHz across predefined beam groups. BR-02: Provide REST/JSON API for external client integration. BR-03: Zero manual processing in standard execution path. BR-04: Maximum 15-minute end-to-end execution SLA. BR-05: Enforce contract-based capacity limits per client and beam group.

open_actions.action: Complete API layer implementation
Execute integration test suite with Planning System

risks: Planning System API stability under load
Third-party Frequency Planning System response time
demands.notes: Phase 3.4 of the Flexible MHz programme. Design review approved on 10 May 2025.

demands.title: Customer Portal — Self-Service Bandwidth Management
demands.description: Web portal enabling enterprise customers to visualise, manage, and adjust their satellite bandwidth allocations in real time without contacting the operations team. Includes dashboards, alerts, and self-service reallocation workflows.
demands.business_requirements: BR-01: Provide real-time visibility of bandwidth consumption per beam and service. BR-02: Enable customers to submit reallocation requests directly from the portal. BR-03: Integrate with BSS/OSS for contract and entitlement data. BR-04: Support role-based access for customer admin and read-only users. BR-05: Provide exportable usage reports.

open_actions.action: Finalise UX wireframes for reallocation workflow
Confirm BSS/OSS API contract data feed specification

risks: BSS/OSS API availability for real-time data
demands.notes: Stakeholder review scheduled for 2 June 2025. UX prototype approved by OceanSat on 8 May.

demands.title: Automated SLA Monitoring & Alerting System
demands.description: Automated system for real-time SLA monitoring across all active satellite services. Generates alerts when performance thresholds are breached and produces automated incident reports for the operations team.
demands.business_requirements: BR-01: Monitor latency, availability, and throughput KPIs in real time. BR-02: Generate alerts within 2 minutes of SLA breach detection. BR-03: Produce automated incident reports with root cause classification. BR-04: Integrate with existing NMS and ticketing systems. BR-05: Provide executive dashboard with SLA compliance scores.

open_actions.action: Complete production environment smoke test
Train operations team on alert management console

risks: Alert fatigue if thresholds not calibrated correctly
demands.notes: Go-live planned for 1 June 2025. Hypercare period of 4 weeks post go-live.

demands.title: LEO/GEO Hybrid Network Orchestration
demands.description: Orchestration layer enabling seamless handover and load balancing between LEO and GEO satellite constellations for aviation customers. Aims to provide consistent connectivity during LEO satellite transitions.
demands.business_requirements: BR-01: Enable automatic handover between LEO and GEO within 500ms. BR-02: Maintain service continuity during constellation transitions. BR-03: Optimise routing based on latency, cost, and availability. BR-04: Support up to 500 concurrent aircraft sessions.

open_actions.action: Complete feasibility assessment for LEO handover latency
Engage AeroLink for requirements workshop

risks: LEO constellation API availability for integration
Regulatory approval for aviation-grade LEO services
demands.notes: Early shaping phase. Concept note under review by Architecture Board.

demands.title: Maritime Connectivity Quality Dashboard
demands.description: Real-time dashboard providing maritime operators with visibility of connectivity quality metrics per vessel — including signal strength, latency, throughput, and beam handover events.
demands.business_requirements: BR-01: Display real-time connectivity KPIs per vessel on a map interface. BR-02: Provide historical trend analysis for the last 30/90 days. BR-03: Alert operators when vessel connectivity degrades below threshold. BR-04: Export reports in PDF and CSV formats.

open_actions.action: Assess data availability from NMS for vessel-level metrics
Present HLA findings to BlueSea steering committee

risks: NMS data granularity may not support vessel-level metrics
GDPR compliance for vessel location data
demands.notes: HLA in progress. Initial architecture options under evaluation.

demands.title: In-Flight Entertainment Bandwidth Optimizer
demands.description: AI-driven bandwidth optimization engine for in-flight entertainment systems. Dynamically allocates available bandwidth across passenger services (streaming, browsing, messaging) based on demand patterns and QoS policies.
demands.business_requirements: BR-01: Allocate bandwidth dynamically based on real-time passenger demand. BR-02: Enforce QoS policies (priority: safety communications > crew > passenger premium > passenger standard). BR-03: Reduce passenger-reported connectivity complaints by 40%. BR-04: Integrate with IFE system APIs for demand signal ingestion.

open_actions.action: Finalise QoS policy matrix with SkyJet
Review AI model training dataset requirements

risks: AI model accuracy in low-bandwidth edge cases
demands.notes: Design phase progressing. Technical design review scheduled 28 May 2025.

demands.title: BSS/OSS Integration — Real-time Provisioning
demands.description: Integration of BSS and OSS platforms to enable real-time automated service provisioning. Eliminates manual provisioning steps and reduces time-to-service from 4 hours to under 10 minutes.
demands.business_requirements: BR-01: Automate end-to-end service provisioning triggered by BSS order events. BR-02: Reduce provisioning time to under 10 minutes. BR-03: Provide real-time provisioning status to customer-facing systems. BR-04: Support rollback on provisioning failure.

open_actions.action: Complete post-implementation review document
Archive all project artefacts to SharePoint

demands.notes: Successfully delivered. Provisioning time reduced to 8 minutes average. Closure report in final review.

demands.title: Satellite Ground Station Redundancy Framework
demands.description: Design and implementation of a geographic redundancy framework for satellite ground stations. Ensures automatic failover within 60 seconds in case of primary ground station outage, maintaining service continuity for all active customers.
demands.business_requirements: BR-01: Achieve automatic failover to secondary ground station within 60 seconds. BR-02: Maintain 99.99% service availability across the redundancy framework. BR-03: Support active-active and active-passive redundancy configurations. BR-04: Zero customer impact during planned maintenance switchovers.

open_actions.action: Identify candidate secondary ground station locations
Define RTO and RPO requirements with Infrastructure team

risks: Geographic constraints for secondary station siting
demands.notes: Critical infrastructure initiative. Triggered by recent industry outage analysis. Executive sponsorship confirmed.

demands.title: Enterprise VSAT Remote Monitoring Platform
demands.description: Centralised remote monitoring platform for GlobalCorp's 200+ VSAT terminals deployed across remote enterprise sites. Provides NOC visibility, predictive maintenance alerts, and automated fault resolution.
demands.business_requirements: BR-01: Monitor all 200+ VSAT terminals from a single NOC dashboard. BR-02: Detect and alert on terminal faults within 5 minutes. BR-03: Provide predictive maintenance indicators based on signal degradation trends. BR-04: Support automated remote reboot and configuration reset for Tier-1 faults.

open_actions.action: Complete load testing for 200 concurrent terminal connections
Resolve SNMP polling timeout issue on legacy terminals

risks: Network latency impact on real-time monitoring accuracy
demands.notes: Integration testing 80% complete. UAT with GlobalCorp scheduled for 15 June 2025.

demands.title: 5G Backhaul via Satellite — Feasibility Study
demands.description: Feasibility study for providing 5G backhaul connectivity via satellite for TowerNet's rural tower network. Evaluates technical viability, latency constraints, capacity requirements, and commercial model for satellite-based 5G backhaul.
demands.business_requirements: BR-01: Assess technical viability of satellite 5G backhaul for latency-sensitive applications. BR-02: Define capacity requirements per tower site based on traffic projections. BR-03: Evaluate LEO vs GEO options for 5G backhaul use cases. BR-04: Produce commercial model recommendations for TowerNet.

open_actions.action: Complete latency benchmark analysis for LEO 5G backhaul
Engage TowerNet for traffic model data sharing

risks: 5G latency requirements may exceed GEO capabilities
TowerNet budget constraints for LEO premium pricing
demands.notes: Feasibility study phase. Results expected by end of June 2025.
